summary: Iteration 2 backend testing for LexManager NEW endpoints only (AI Drafting Studio + Drafts CRUD + Documents upload/viewer). Created /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py with 23 focused tests. ALL 23 TESTS PASSED (100%). Tested against the public ingress URL (EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). Claude Haiku 4.5 via emergentintegrations/EMERGENT_LLM_KEY responded in ~11s for draft-from-facts and produced a well-structured Indian-legal-English legal notice.

minor.issue: The 413 size threshold is len(base64) > MAX_DOC_SIZE * 1.4 = ~14MB, not the stated 10MB. The error message 'File too large (max 10MB)' is misleading — a 13MB base64 string would be accepted despite the message. Either tighten the threshold to MAX_DOC_SIZE exactly, or update the error message to reflect the actual ~14MB cap.
Version increments on every PUT even if no fields actually changed. Consider only bumping version when content changes, or exposing a force-bump flag.

matter_id in DraftFromFactsIn is accepted but never validated against the caller's lawyer_id — an attacker could theoretically pass someone else's matter_id, but since it's not persisted here, impact is low. Consider validating ownership if matter_id is ever used downstream.

No validation that matter_id actually belongs to the calling lawyer — a malicious client could attach a document to any matter_id string. The doc is still scoped to the uploader's lawyer_id so listing is safe, but it creates orphan/garbage data. Recommend validating matter ownership before insert.
Same as above — matter_id is not verified to belong to the caller's lawyer_id before creating the draft.

action_items: (Optional) Fix 413 threshold or error message mismatch in POST /api/documents (currently rejects at ~14MB, error says 10MB).
(Optional) Validate matter_id ownership on POST /api/drafts and POST /api/documents to prevent cross-tenant data pollution.
(Optional) Consider only bumping draft version when content actually changes.
critical_code_review_comments: Emergentintegrations LlmChat is invoked correctly with anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 and EMERGENT_LLM_KEY — Claude responded in ~11s with a properly formatted Indian legal notice.
All new endpoints correctly enforce Bearer-token auth (401 without, verified for both /ai/*, /drafts, /documents).
Multi-tenancy is correctly enforced on /drafts and /documents via lawyer_id scoping — a second user cannot list, fetch, or mutate the first user's drafts/documents (returns 404).
GET /api/documents (list) correctly projects away base64; GET /api/documents/{id} includes base64. Contract honoured.
POST /api/documents (create) correctly strips base64 from response.
Draft version increments correctly on PUT (verified v1 → v2).
MongoDB _id is properly stripped on every new endpoint's response (verified across list/detail/create/update).
413 response is returned for oversized base64 payloads (verified with 15MB payload).
Route ordering has no conflicts between /api/drafts/{id}, /api/documents/{id} and list endpoints.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing demo@lex.in user's seeded matter for draft/document creation. All test artifacts prefixed TEST_ and cleaned up (drafts & docs deleted at end of each flow).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both new feature suites (AI Drafting + Documents) are fully green. Regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py takes ~20s (AI calls dominate, ~11s + ~3s). If iterating on AI: expect Claude Haiku 4.5 latency of 8-15s per call; keep test timeouts ≥40s. The tiny-PNG base64 constant TINY_PNG_B64 in the test file is handy for quick document upload smoke tests. MAX_DOC_SIZE threshold is actually ~14MB (10MB * 1.4) despite the error message saying 10MB — main agent may want to align these.
